definition: Physical and chemical degradation of surface and ground waters from mining-related activities: sediment discharge, acid rock drainage (ARD), heavy metal leaching, process chemical spills, and tailings seepage.
key_kpis: Water use intensity (m3/t); freshwater withdrawal vs discharge ratio; heavy metal concentrations (As, Hg, Pb, Cd); effluent compliance %; tailings seepage rate; pH.

definition: Water withdrawal or consumption exceeding natural replenishment, concentrated in arid or water-stressed basins. Groundwater aquifer drawdown is a particular concern for large-scale mining and processing.
key_kpis: Water withdrawal intensity (m3/t); share of portfolio in High/Extremely High BWS basins; aquifer decline rate (cm/yr); water recycle rate (%).

definition: Fine-grained processed waste stored in large tailings storage facilities (TSFs). Failure or chronic seepage releases contaminated slurry containing heavy metals and process reagents into downstream rivers, soils and groundwater.
key_kpis: Volume of tailings per t ore (t/t); count of TSFs and GISTM-conformant %; number and consequence class of TSFs; seepage rate; tailings-related incidents/yr.

definition: Solid and liquid waste streams (slag, flue dust, sludge, spent refractories, process fluids) whose hazardous properties and management quality determine dispersion of contamination into water, soil and air.
key_kpis: Tonnage hazardous waste generated; chemical concentration; water pH; seepage volume; land footprint; water-to-ore ratio; circular economy metric.

definition: Decline or local extinction of species caused by mining and associated infrastructure: habitat destruction, fragmentation, pollution, invasive species, hunting/fishing pressure from access roads.
key_kpis: IUCN-listed threatened/endemic species within buffer zones; hectares of critical habitat disturbed; % sites with biodiversity management plans.

definition: Degradation of terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems from mining: deforestation, soil erosion, hydrological disruption, and loss of ecosystem services (carbon, water regulation, nutrient cycling).
key_kpis: Area disturbed/converted (ha); deforestation (km2/yr); % sites with restoration plans; ecosystem water quality indicators; proximity to protected areas.

definition: Occupational and ambient noise exposure from mining equipment (blasting, drilling, haul trucks, crushers) and processing (furnace fans, shredders). Exceedances trigger hearing loss and community grievances.
key_kpis: dBA 8-hr TWA; workforce exposed >85 dBA; peak noise levels; number of exceedances/violations.

definition: Point-source stack emissions from smelters/refineries and fugitive dust from mining. Key pollutants: SO2, NOx, PM10/PM2.5, heavy metal fumes (As, Pb, Cd, Hg).
key_kpis: SO2/NOx (t/yr); PM10/PM2.5 ambient (mg/m3); dust fallout rate; heavy metal air concentration; number of air quality exceedances.

definition: Soil contamination from hazardous spills, tailings seepage, and historical heavy metal accumulation. Alters chemistry, reducing agricultural/ecological use, and creates long-term legacy liability.
key_kpis: Significant spill incidents (Cat 3-5); heavy metal soil concentrations (mg/kg); rehabilitation rate (%); hazardous waste tonnage; seepage well deviations.
likelihood_indicator: Soil vulnerability (pH, SOC, CEC) blended with EPI Heavy Metals

definition: CO2, CH4 and N2O emissions from fuel combustion, electricity use, process emissions (smelting), and fugitive methane (coal/oil & gas).
key_kpis: Carbon intensity (tCO2e/t product); energy intensity; absolute Scope 1+2 (tCO2e); methane leak rate.

definition: Harm to worker and community health from hazardous exposure, unsafe conditions, and environmental contamination attributable to operations.
key_kpis: TRIR; fatality rate; number of community health incidents; exposure exceedances.
severity_indicator: Premature mortality from ambient air pollution

definition: Disruption of lives and livelihoods from displacement tied to mining activity and associated infrastructure.
key_kpis: Land-related grievances; resettlement complaints; use-of-force incidents; # affected households.

definition: Failure risk from technical and climate-driven hazards causing long-term disruption to infrastructure and downstream communities.
key_kpis: Extensometer/inclinometer readings; dam stability alerts; flood/seismic exposure.

risk_type: Damage to aesthetics / cultural heritage sites
definition: Damage to archaeological sites, historic architecture, and landscapes of cultural significance from operations.
key_kpis: Count of heritage sites within concession; documented disturbance incidents; community grievances.

definition: Negligent, incorrect, or illegal discarding of solid, liquid, or hazardous materials in unauthorized locations violating environmental regulations.
key_kpis: Unauthorized discharge volumes; EIFR; regulatory compliance deviation; discharge quality variance.
